content.text: I’ve mapped the project files and filtered out unrelated materials. I’ll now gather the core status, risks, and roadmap details to draft your briefing.

content.content:
Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

content.text: I’ve compiled the full status picture from your project files. I’ll now draft the health briefing for leadership.

input.content:
NovaBridge Leadership — Project Helios Health Briefing
Date: 2026-07-11
Prepared for: Leadership Team
Project: Project Helios (Client: Meridian Group)
Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)
Status: ON TRACK (minor risks)

================================================================
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
================================================================
Project Helios is currently On Track with minor risks. Core infrastructure (API gateway, architecture, dev environment) is complete. The remaining Q2 carryover work — data ingestion module (85%) and client dashboard v1 (60%) — is in progress toward July 18-22 targets.

The next major milestone is Integration Test Sign-off on 2026-07-25, coinciding with the Product Demo for Meridian stakeholders. Two active blockers and several overdue items require close management to avoid Q3 launch slippage, which would trigger Section 7.1 penalty review per contract.

================================================================
2. CURRENT PROGRESS
================================================================
Milestone Progress (as of Week of 2026-07-06):
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered) - RESTful, OAuth 2.0, rate-limited 1k req/min
- Client dashboard: 60% complete (React-based, RBAC)
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Q2 Deliverables Checklist:
  [DONE] Requirements documentation v1.0
  [DONE] System architecture design
  [DONE] API gateway implementation
  [DONE] Development environment setup
  [DONE] Data schema finalization
  [IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module - target 2026-07-18
  [IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 - target 2026-07-22
  [OVERDUE] Integration test suite setup - was due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20
  [OVERDUE] UAT environment provisioning - was due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access
  [OVERDUE] Performance benchmarking report - was due 2026-07-05, owner Derek Okafor

Sprint 14 (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18) Task Status:
- HEL-241 Dashboard: data table component (Fatima Al-Rashid) - In Progress, due 7/14
- HEL-242 Dashboard: chart rendering module (Tomas Rivera) - In Progress, due 7/16
- HEL-243 Data ingestion: date format normalization (Amir Hassan) - Blocked
- HEL-244 Integration test suite: auth flows (Kenji Watanabe) - Done
- HEL-245 Integration test suite: data pipeline (Kenji Watanabe) - In Progress, due 7/18
- HEL-246 UAT environment: server provisioning (Nadia Petrov) - At Risk, due 7/16
- HEL-247 Performance benchmark: dashboard load (Derek Okafor) - Not Started, due 7/18
- HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal coordination (Raj Patel) - In Progress, due 7/14

QA Testing Log - Sprint 14 (Kenji Watanabe):
- Authentication Flows: 4/4 PASS (TC-401 to TC-404)
- Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion: 5/7 PASS
  - PASS: CSV 10k, JSON 10k, XML 5k, duplicate detection, ISO 8601 normalization
  - FAIL: TC-505 Date normalization MM/DD/YYYY (HEL-D-014) - HIGH severity, affects ~40% of Meridian source data
  - FAIL: TC-507 Large file 500k records (HEL-D-015) - HIGH severity, memory overflow crash at ~210k records
- Dashboard: 1/4 complete (data table rendering PASS, chart rendering In Progress, load benchmark NOT RUN)
- Overall: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL open, 3 not yet run.

Recent Decisions (Sync with Meridian 2026-07-08):
- Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25, must include LIVE data ingestion (per James Whitfield), not mock data
- Meridian to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
- UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28) - already consumed buffer

Architecture Status: Stable. Data ingestion layer (CSV/JSON/XML, batch/streaming), transformation engine (Spark, 500k rec/min target), API gateway, dashboard, PostgreSQL + S3 storage. End-to-end encryption (TLS 1.3, AES-256), stateless scaling.

================================================================
3. KEY RISKS (Risk Register Last Reviewed: 2026-07-07)
================================================================
HIGH PRIORITY:

R-01: Meridian source data quality issues - Likelihood: High, Impact: High, Score: 9
  Status: Active, confirmed blocker Sprint 14. Inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY).
  Owner: Amir Hassan. Mitigation: Awaiting Meridian sample data due 7/15. Defect HEL-D-014.

R-02: GeoSync license not renewed before demo - Medium/High, Score 6
  Status: Active, legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14. Blocks map functionality for demo.
  Owner: Raj Patel

R-03: Dashboard performance on large datasets - Medium/High, Score 6
  Concern raised by Meridian, load time target <2s for 100k records not yet benchmarked.
  Task HEL-247 not started. Owner: Derek Okafor

R-04: UAT timeline slippage - High/Medium, Score 6
  1-week extension already consumed. UAT env provisioning blocked by Meridian IT access. New UAT window Aug 17-28.
  Owner: Sarah Chen

MEDIUM:

R-06: Meridian IT environment access delays - Medium/Medium, Score 4
  Awaiting IT infrastructure diagram (owner Priya Kapoor, due 7/12) and UAT access by 7/19.

R-05: Key person dependency: Derek Okafor - Low/High, Score 3
  No backup tech lead identified. Critical for performance benchmarking and license resolution.

LOW/MONITORED:
R-07: Scope creep from Meridian feature requests (3 informal requests logged)
R-08: Cloud cost overrun
R-09: Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)

Additional Technical Risk:
HEL-D-015 Large file ingestion memory overflow >200k records - crashes at ~210k. Under investigation, threatens throughput target of 500k records/min sustained.

================================================================
4. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS (Roadmap Draft 2026-07-06)
================================================================
JULY 2026 - Complete System Integration:
- Finalize data ingestion module: target 2026-07-18
- Complete client dashboard v1: target 2026-07-22
- Integration test sign-off: target 2026-07-25

JULY 25, 2026 - Product Demo for Meridian:
- Live data ingestion demo using Meridian sample data
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
- Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)
- Prereqs: Resolve GeoSync license, fix date normalization OR receive clean dataset, complete dashboard charting, benchmark performance

AUGUST 17-28, 2026 - User Acceptance Testing:
- Deploy to UAT environment (currently At Risk)
- Support Meridian user testing sessions
- Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)
- Dependencies: Meridian IT infra diagram by 7/12, UAT access by 7/19, draft demo agenda sent by Sarah Chen by 7/18

AUGUST-SEPTEMBER - Performance Hardening:
- Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
- Ingestion throughput: 500k records/min sustained
- External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

SEPTEMBER - Launch Preparation:
- Production environment setup and runbook documentation
- Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
- Final client training sessions

Target Launch Date: 2026-10-01
Penalty Clause Risk: UAT slippage >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 Feature Additions (subject to change control):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

================================================================
5. ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
================================================================
1. IMMEDIATE - GeoSync License Renewal (Due 2026-07-14):
   Legal review must close in 3 days or demo is at risk. Owner Raj Patel needs Legal prioritization. Leadership may need to escalate with vendor.

2. IMMEDIATE - Data Quality Blocker & Meridian Dependency:
   Date format issue affects 40% of source data. Fix pending Meridian clean dataset by 7/15. Also large-file memory overflow (HEL-D-015) has no ETA. Needs tech lead focus - consider pairing Amir Hassan with additional backend support.

3. HIGH - Overdue Performance Benchmarking:
   Report was due 7/05, task HEL-247 not started. Dashboard concerns already raised by Meridian sponsor. This is now demo-critical and launch-critical. Derek Okafor is overloaded and is a single point of failure.

4. HIGH - UAT Environment At Risk:
   Overdue since 7/01. Blocked by Meridian IT but also internal task HEL-246 At Risk. Two external dependencies (infra diagram due 7/12, access by 7/19) must be tracked daily. If UAT slips beyond Aug 28, launch moves to Q4 and triggers penalty review.

5. MEDIUM - Resource & Ownership Risk:
   Key person dependency on Derek Okafor for both performance and GeoSync coordination. Recommend identifying backup tech lead immediately.
   Three overdue Q2 items + two active HIGH defects suggests Sprint 14 may be over-committed.

6. MEDIUM - Scope Creep:
   3 informal feature requests logged from Meridian, not yet escalated. Given tight timeline and consumed UAT buffer, leadership should enforce strict change control for proposed Q3 additions until integration sign-off is complete.

================================================================
6. RECOMMENDATIONS
================================================================
- Escalate GeoSync legal review today.
- Daily check-in with Meridian IT lead (Priya Kapoor) until infra diagram and UAT access delivered.
- Reassign or add resource to HEL-247 performance benchmark to meet pre-demo expectations.
- Allocate additional engineering support to investigate HEL-D-015 memory overflow before it becomes a launch blocker.
- Brief leadership on contingency if Meridian sample data (due 7/15) is delayed again - consider fallback conversion script.
- Freeze scope until after July 25 demo; formalize change control process for Q3 proposed features.
- Document backup for Derek Okafor role.

================================================================
END OF BRIEFING
================================================================

content.text:
Comprehensive project health briefing has been prepared and saved as **project_health_briefing.txt**.

The briefing includes:

**1. Current Progress:** On Track (minor risks) - Data ingestion 85%, API gateway 100% delivered, dashboard 60%, integration testing 40%. Sprint 14 QA results (10/15 tests executed, 2 HIGH fails: date normalization HEL-D-014 affecting 40% of Meridian data, and memory overflow HEL-D-015 at 210k records). 3 overdue Q2 items noted.

**2. Key Risks:** 9 risks from risk register, prioritized - R-01 data quality (Score 9, active blocker), R-02 GeoSync license renewal (due 7/14), R-03 dashboard performance (<2s for 100k records not benchmarked), R-04 UAT slippage (buffer already consumed), plus key-person dependency on Derek Okafor, Meridian IT access delays, and scope creep.

**3. Q3 Commitments:** July integration completion (7/18-25), Product Demo July 25 (must include LIVE data per contract Sec 9.3 and Meridian sponsor), UAT Aug 17-28, Performance Hardening (500k rec/min, pen test), Launch Prep with target launch 10/01/2026 - with penalty clause Sec 7.1 if UAT slips >2 weeks into Q4.

**4. Leadership Attention Issues:** Immediate escalation needed for GeoSync legal review, Meridian dependency for clean dataset due 7/15, overdue performance benchmarking, UAT environment At Risk, resource single-point-of-failure, and informal scope creep requiring strict change control.
